title: Vocis Kenit
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Vocis_Kenit.jpg]] |name=Vocis Kenit |homeworld=Somewhere in the [[Hallu sector/Legends|Hallu sector]]''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'' |birth=Prior to [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]] |death=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Fair |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=[[Vocis Kenit's brother]] |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You will go to the Ringali Shell to locate the ''Far Orbit'' and carry out your mission. You will bring the ''Far Orbit'' to heel or pay the price.|Grand Vizier Sate Pestage briefs Kenit on his mission|{{WEGCite|story=The Capture of Coh Veshiv|book=The Far Orbit Project}}}} '''Vocis Kenit''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who entered the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]] sometime before [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]]. Rising to the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] due to his frequent reporting on the flaws of other [[Officer/Legends|officers]], Kenit was assigned the [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''. In 0 ABY, while the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] was being fought by the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], some of the crew of the ''Far Orbit'', led by [[Executive officer/Legends|second officer]] [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij]], [[Mutiny on the Far Orbit|mutinied]] and seized the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] from Kenit and the loyal Imperials aboard. Briefly [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] by his superiors because of his failure in command after the crew of the ''Far Orbit'' let him go, Kenit was then assigned to track the ''Far Orbit'', which had become an Alliance [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]] vessel. Although Kenit came close to capturing the ship by manipulating them into [[Battle of the Solar Terrors|attacking]] a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Solar Terrors|band]] and later drawing them into a [[Battle in Brentaal space|confrontation]] with an Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], Kenit's efforts ultimately came to naught, and he was [[Death/Legends|executed]] by [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself for his failure. ==Biography== ===Early career=== A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Vocis Kenit was born in the [[Hallu sector/Legends|Hallu sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. He had at least [[Vocis Kenit's brother|one sibling]]. Kenit enlisted in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]] by [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]], during the Imperial Navy's first major expansion, and, upon serving for two [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Kenit attended the [[Melford Star Academy|Imperial Naval Academy]] on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Lianna/Legends|Lianna]]. Kenit graduated from there as an [[Officer/Legends|officer]], and he was assigned to the ''[[Semqi Pefis]]'', an Imperial [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. On the [[Starship/Legends|ship]], Kenit brought the careers of several higher-ranking officers under scrutiny by reporting their [[Politics/Legends|political]] faults. Kenit was promoted at the end of his tour of duty on the ''Semqi Pefis'', and was transferred to the [[Thomquizzar Naval Base]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Like always, Kenit performed well, intelligently executing his duties while identifying the faults of his superior Imperial officers. He rose through the ranks in like fashion: undermining the careers of his superiors and rivals, and receiving a series of promotions and transfers for his efforts. Indeed, many of Kenit's superiors found rewarding the officer to be the easiest and safest way to get rid of him. In time, Kenit was given the command of the new [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'', serving as its [[Captain/Legends|captain]] as he and the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] rooted out [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] nests in the Outer Rim. [[File:Kenit_kicked_out.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Kenit and his loyalists are kicked off the ''Far Orbit'' after the mutiny.]] Kenit was a brutal captain for the crew of the ''Far Orbit''; his methods of maintaining discipline were harsh, while his treatment of captured enemies was even worse. Many of the personnel and officers on the ship disliked him, including the [[Executive officer/Legends|second in command]], [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij]]. One dissenting [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Vocis Kenit)|officer]] on the ''Far Orbit'' was reported to the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]], and mysteriously [[Death/Legends|died]] later. Three pirate lords and many smaller pirating organizations fell to the ''Far Orbit'' because of Kenit's cunning and his ability to anticipate movements. Kenit and the ''Far Orbit'' tracked one pirate organization known as the [[Tarnished Blades|Silent Blades]] for [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]], and the Captain finally forced the pirates into a stand-off at an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]]: while the ''Far Orbit'' could not enter the belt, the holed-up pirates could not get out. Kenit ignored Vedij's suggestion to call for reinforcements, as Kenit desired the glory of victory for himself. Thus, Kenit had the ''Far Orbit''{{'s}} [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] complement [[Battle of the Silent Blades|attack]] the pirates in the asteroid field. The starfighter assault was successful and destroyed the majority of the pirates, although the entire TIE force was lost, including Vedij's brother, [[Elon Vedij|Elon]]. ===Mutiny=== {{Quote|We will hunt you down. Your families will be executed. And you, Commander, will be subjected to the slowest torture that can be devised.''"
"''Perhaps, but you will not live to see it.|Vocis Kenit and Dhas Vedij, as the former is kicked off his frigate|}} Due to the death of his brother and his disillusionment with the Empire, Vedij led a [[Mutiny on the Far Orbit|mutiny]] against Kenit and the loyal Imperials after Kenit's men arrested a [[Unidentified Alderaanian shiphand|shiphand]] for being from [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], a world that was pro-Alliance until the Empire [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] it with the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|DS-1 Orbital Battle Station]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].{{Film|IV}} When Vedij used rumors about the arresting of the shiphand to outrage the crew, two-thirds of Kenit's command followed the first officer in the mutiny and ousted Kenit from power. The Captain was personally sent off the ship by Vedij in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] along with those crew members who were against the mutiny. Kenit was later recovered by the Imperials. He gave a report on what had happened and was imprisoned to await punishment. His political enemies, slighted by Kenit over the years in his rise through the ranks, arranged for a [[Court-martial/Legends|court-martial]] due to his failure of command. [[File:Vocis_Kenit_speaks_to_Sate_Pestage.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Vocis Kenit communicates with Sate Pestage.]] After a few weeks, Kenit was sentenced via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] by [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], who was close to [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Because the ruler of the Galactic Empire had been angered when he heard of the mutiny, Pestage was leading the investigation into the matter, and gave Kenit a suspended sentence of death. The carrying out of the sentence would wait until Kenit's new assignment, the capturing of the ''Far Orbit'', could be completed. If Kenit failed, he would die, while success would let him live. Kenit took on the mission and searched for Vedij and the ''Far Orbit'' in the Outer Rim with the Imperial frigate [[Stalwart (EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate)|''Stalwart'']]. He also placed [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] on the heads of his former crew. ===Hunt for the ''Far Orbit''=== {{Quote|Attention ''Far Orbit'', this is the Imperial frigate ''Stalwart'', commanded by Captain Vocis Kenit. Your ship and her crew are under arrest for mutiny, treason, piracy, and conspiracy to overthrow the empire. If you surrender the traitor Dhas Vedij, your cooperation will be considered during your trial.|Kenit hails the ''Far Orbit''|{{WEGCite|story=The Trap (adventure scenario)|book=The Far Orbit Project}}}} Pestage later contacted Kenit during his search, and the Imperial captain was informed that the ''Far Orbit'' had moved to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' [[Ringali Shell]]. There, Vedij and the crew—who had enlisted as [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]] with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as part of their [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] against the Empire—had [[Capture of Coh Veshiv|captured]] [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] [[Coh Veshiv]] near the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]]. {{RPGseed}} Authorized to use extreme measures to capture the ''Far Orbit'', Kenit came up with a plan to discredit the ship and [[Poison/Legends|poison]] the public's opinion of the frigate by announcing that it was a privateering vessel. In the Ringali Shell, Kenit forced a [[Capture of the Solar Terrors|captured]] pirate band known as the [[Solar Terrors]] and their leader, [[Venslas Beeli]], to attack civilian targets near the worlds of [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]], [[Rhinnal/Legends|Rhinnal]], and Esseles under the guise of Alliance privateers. During their set of raids, Beeli's fleet of ships retired to a deep space position where the ''Stalwart'' and Kenit resided, and Imperial patrols were restrained from increasing their activity in order to let the ''Far Orbit'' engage the pirates, because Vedij and the crew wanted to destroy the pirates to maintain their reputation. In the end, Vedij and the ''Far Orbit'' indeed [[Battle of the Solar Terrors|destroyed]] the pirates.{{WEGCite|story=The Rival|book=The Far Orbit Project}} [[File:Battle_over_the_Far_Orbit.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Vocis Kenit's taskforce draws near to the ''Far Orbit''.]] Kenit's next move was to set a trap for the ''Far Orbit''. Using the seemingly poorly defended [[Mark III bulk transport]] [[SPF 14897]] as bait, Kenit lured in the ''Far Orbit''. The privateer crew conducted a raid on the transport; however, the cargo hold contained three [[Squad/Legends|squads]] of elite [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], who attempted a counter-boarding operation against the frigate. With the ''Far Orbit'' stuck fending off the boarders, Kenit led a small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to capture the vessel. Kenit hailed the ''Far Orbit'', announcing that they would be arrested for [[Treason/Legends|treason]], and in the event that Vedij was handed to the Imperials, the crew would be better off. Kenit's [[Unidentified Imperial task force|fleet]]—two [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat]]s, the [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruiser]] [[Hammer (Strike-class)|''Hammer'']], the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] [[Claw (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|''Claw'']], and the ''Stalwart''—[[Battle in Brentaal space|attacked]] the ''Far Orbit'', with capital ships pounding the ''Far Orbit'' with [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] fire as they launched their TIE fighters. Due to its lack of ion cannons, Kenit's ''Stalwart'' gripped the frigate with its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] to hold the ''Far Orbit'' still. Despite the odds stacked against them, the crew of the ''Far Orbit'' still prevailed, using the frigate's own tractor beams to grip on to passing TIEs and use them as shields against the Skiprays that had flanked the EF76. The ''Far Orbit'' then bore down on the ''Claw'', whose [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] were keeping the ''Far Orbit'' from jumping to hyperspace, and the privateering vessel destroyed one of the projectors in the following engagement. Now free to jump, the heavily damaged ''Far Orbit'' escaped, leaving Kenit's mission in ruins. {{Endgame}} ===Demise=== {{Quote|Captain, your efforts under the instruction of my good friend, the Grand Vizier, have come to my personal attention. I understand the ''Far Orbit'' has acquired a new prize.|Emperor Palpatine addresses Kenit for the first and last time|}} That same year, as the ''Far Orbit'' evaded Kenit's efforts to capture it, the frigate [[Capture of the Emperor's Will|took]] the [[Star Galleon-class frigate|''Star Galleon''-class frigate]] ''[[Emperor's Will]]'', a great prize loaded with treasure. Among the wealth was a single [[Corusca gem/Legends|Corusca gem]],{{WEGCite|story=The Grand Prize|book=The Far Orbit Project}} which had unique tracking abilities. Because his brother was a [[gemologist]], Kenit was aware of the Corusca gem's special properties, so he felt confident that he would finally be able to capture the frigate. However, Kenit was summoned to the holopod and confronted by not Pestage, but Palpatine himself on the feed, who was angered at the loss of the ''Emperor's Will''. Kenit was executed by Palpatine immediately by [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Choke|choking]] from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for his failure to locate the ''Far Orbit'', and the secret of tracking the ship died with him. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Do not fail your Emperor, Captain. You are well aware of the rewards of success…and the price of failure.|Grand Vizier Sate Pestage addresses Kenit|}} Kenit was a fair-skinned man who had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and sported a moustache. The man was not particularly brilliant when it came to military operations, although he was a decent tactician good at anticipating the moves of the enemy. Kenit rose up the ranks via his political acumen rather than his military performance. At each post, the Captain looked for political faults in his superiors and brought their careers under scrutiny as he completed his missions intelligently; Kenit's habit created multiple enemies in the Imperial ranks. Kenit's cunning and technical excellence, as well as his undying devotion to the New Order, allowed his rise. As the commander of the ''Far Orbit'', the Captain was an authoritarian and a petty despot, causing many of the crew to dislike him. Kenit treated captured prisoners even worse than he treated his men. Kenit's treatment of his men, and his pursuit of glory and promotion in his battle with the Silent Blades, compelled his crew to mutiny, an event that Kenit never predicted. Kenit considered Vedij a traitor. The officer was afraid of failing his superiors in the search for the ''Far Orbit'', as he knew the consequence was death. During the attempt to capture the ''Far Orbit'' with his fleet, Kenit had the ''Stalwart'' stay back from the immediate fighting as he wanted to stay out of harm's way. ==Behind the scenes== Vocis Kenit was first mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'', by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]]. The book was a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and was a sequel of sorts to the previous publication ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', carrying on that book's theme by following the ''Far Orbit''{{'s}} adventures. In ''The Far Orbit Project'', Vocis Kenit serves as the main villain in his attempts to capture the privateering vessel, and he appears in the adventure scenarios ''[[The Capture of Coh Veshiv]]'', ''[[The Rival]]'', and [[The Trap (adventure scenario)|''The Trap'']], which were all included in the book. In ''The Far Orbit Project'', Kenit's attempts to capture the ''Far Orbit'' can take numerous paths, as the adventure changes according to how the players and the gamemaster—the person who manages the adventure—do it. The pirates that Kenit sends out to discredit the frigate can be used as the bait to allow Kenit's fleet to catch up to the EF76, instead of SPF 14897. During the battle between the fleet and Vedij's force, Vedij can surrender to Kenit in return for safe passage for his men. However, if this happens, Kenit plans to double-cross Vedij and capture the ship, although the ''Far Orbit'' escapes as Vedij shuttles over to the ''Stalwart''. This article documents the path that represents the players handling challenges in the way that results in the best overall outcome for the privateers of the ''Far Orbit'', as well as the way that allows all of the adventure scenarios to fit within the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] continuity. ==Appearances== *{{WEGCite|story=The Capture of Coh Veshiv|book=The Far Orbit Project}} {{1st}} *{{WEGCite|story=The Rival|book=The Far Orbit Project}} *{{WEGCite|story=The Trap (adventure scenario)|book=The Far Orbit Project}} ==Sources== *''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kenit, Vocis}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy captains of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
